articles.title: Dhule Heat Wave: Temperature in Dhule Reaches 43 to 44 Degrees Celsius; Banana and Papaya Farmers Hit Hard - Lokshahi English News

articles.full_text:
Temperature in Dhule Reaches 43 to 44 Degrees Celsius
Dhule Heat Wave: The intensity of the heat is increasing day by day. The heat is becoming more intense, causing discomfort to citizens. Going outside during the afternoon has become difficult. With a significant rise in temperature, the effects of the heat are increasing. Currently, the intensity of the heat is growing day by day.
In some areas, the temperature may rise further. Even in rural areas, the impact of the heat has increased. The heat has risen to such an extent that significant damage to crops is being observed. Against this backdrop, banana and papaya farmers in Dhule district are facing a major setback due to the rising heat.
With the temperature reaching 43 to 44 degrees Celsius in the district, newly planted banana saplings are burning. The leaves of the plants are wilting, and many saplings are drying up completely. This is affecting the quality of production, and farmers are experiencing increased financial losses. Farmers are finding themselves in economic distress.
Short Summary
Extreme temperatures of 43–44°C in Dhule district
Significant impact on banana and papaya farmers
Newly planted saplings are burning and drying up
Increased costs due to replanting
Impact on quality of production and harvesting
Farmers are temporarily seeking shade solutions
Farmers are in economic distress
